SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.io.vsam

Class AbstractVsamFile

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.io.AbstractFile
com.nib.commons.io.vsam.AbstractVsamFile
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractEntrySequentialFile, AbstractKeySequentialFile, AbstractRecordRelativeFile
public abstract class AbstractVsamFile extends AbstractFile
AbstractVsamFile serves as a base class for implementing various types of VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file operations. It extends the AbstractFile class, adding functionality tailored for VSAM handling, such as opening, writing, reading, and caching file operations.

  • Field Details

    • ioapi

      protected IVsamApi ioapi
      Represents the IO interface implementation specific to VSAM file operations. This variable is designed to interact with the underlying storage layers. It is initialized and managed within the lifecycle of an AbstractVsamFile instance. The specific implementation of the interface IVsamApi can vary depending on the requirements for VSAM file interactions such as caching and IO operations.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractVsamFile

      public AbstractVsamFile(Context ctx, FileDescriptor fileDescriptor)
      Constructor for creating an instance of AbstractVsamFile with the specified context and file descriptor.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the context in which the file operation is executed
      fileDescriptor - the descriptor of the file to be used

    • AbstractVsamFile

      public AbstractVsamFile(Context ctx, String ddName)
      Constructor for creating an instance of AbstractVsamFile with the specified context and dataset name (ddName).
      Parameters:
      ctx - the context in which the file operation is executed
      ddName - the dataset name to be associated with the file

  • Method Details

    • cached

      public AbstractVsamFile cached()
      Marks the current instance as cached.
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractVsamFile with caching enabled

    • initIoApi

      public abstract void initIoApi()
      Initializes the Input/Output API for the VSAM file. This method must be implemented by subclasses to provide the specific logic and configuration required for setting up the I/O API.

      It is typically called during the initialization of a VSAM file instance to ensure that the necessary API or backend is correctly configured before performing any file operations.

      Subclasses should use this method to handle any required setup, such as opening connections, setting up buffers, or configuring file access parameters.

    • open

      public FileIOResponse open(OpenMode openMode, LockMode lockMode) throws FileIOException
      Opens the file with the specified open mode and lock mode. This method initializes the Input/Output API for the file and checks whether the file descriptor is cached. If cached, it utilizes the cache through the cache manager.
      Overrides:
      open in class AbstractFile
      Parameters:
      openMode - the mode in which the file is to be opened (e.g., read, write, append)
      lockMode - the locking mode for the file (e.g., shared, exclusive)
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse indicating the success or failure of the file open operation
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an error occurs while attempting to open the file

    • write

      public abstract FileIOResponse write()
      Writes the record to the file associated with this instance.
      Specified by:
      write in class AbstractFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse representing the result of the write operation, including status, any generated errors, or other relevant details.

    • rewrite

      public abstract FileIOResponse rewrite()
      Rewrites the current record or data associated with the file. This method provides functionality to overwrite the existing contents without creating a new record or entry. The specific implementation of this operation will depend on the underlying file system or I/O system being used.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the rewrite operation, including status information or any errors that may have occurred.

    • rewrite

      public FileIOResponse rewrite(AbstractNField from)
      Rewrites the provided record in the file using the specified field data.
      Parameters:
      from - the AbstractNField containing the data to be used for the rewrite operation
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the rewrite operation, including status information or any errors that may have occurred

    • readNext

      public FileIOResponse readNext()
      Reads the next record from the file. If the file supports sequential access, this method retrieves the next available record in the sequence without any additional update details.
      Overrides:
      readNext in class AbstractFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse representing the result of the read operation, including status, any encountered errors, or other relevant information.

    • readNext

      public FileIOResponse readNext(boolean update)
      Reads the next record or data from the file in sequential order and prepares for update.
      Specified by:
      readNext in class AbstractFile
      Parameters:
      update - a boolean indicating whether the current operation should prepare for update
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse representing the outcome of the read operation, including status information, encountered errors, or other relevant details

    • readPrev

      public FileIOResponse readPrev()
      Reads the previous record from the file in sequential order.
      Overrides:
      readPrev in class AbstractFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse representing the result of the read operation, including status information, encountered errors, or other relevant details.

    • readPrev

      public FileIOResponse readPrev(boolean update)
      Reads the previous record from the file in sequential order and prepares for update.
      Specified by:
      readPrev in class AbstractFile
      Parameters:
      update - a boolean indicating whether the current operation should prepare for update
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse representing the outcome of the read operation, including status information, encountered errors, or other relevant details

    • readSequential

      public abstract FileIOResponse readSequential(KeyIs direction, boolean update)
      Reads records from the file sequentially in the direction specified by the dir parameter. This method also allows specifying whether subsequent operations should prepare for updates.
      Parameters:
      direction - the direction in which records are to be read, represented by the KeyIs object
      update - a boolean indicating whether the current operation should prepare for updates
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the read operation, including status information, encountered errors, or other relevant details

    • close

      public FileIOResponse close() throws FileIOException
      Closes the file associated with this instance and releases any resources being used. This method ensures that all file operations are properly completed and the file is safely closed. It is typically invoked when the file is no longer needed.
      Overrides:
      close in class AbstractFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse indicating the result of the close operation, including success status or any errors that may have occurred.
      Throws:
      FileIOException - if an error occurs while attempting to close the file.

    • delete

      public abstract FileIOResponse delete()
      Deletes the current record.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the delete operation, including status information or any errors that may have occurred.
